title: douglasia ochotensis subsp. arctica
intro: Douglasia ochotensis subsp. arctica, also known as the Arctic douglasia or Arctic sweetroot, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of iridoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Douglasia ochotensis subsp. arctica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used in some traditional herbal remedies to treat various conditions.
